what_it_does: This noticeboard block defines the 'context envelope' — the unit that gets stored and sent for an annotation or comment — by listing what must be included, inferring the structure that goal implies, and adding proposed features such as intent tags, status, versioning, and audit trail.
observations: The file is a block-type noticeboard item in the dragnet-development channel, authored by ch-3mpkjg3r, and links to board://item-11da929d as part_of.

The SPECIFIED section states the envelope must store and send the annotation, the annotated content, context, element IDs/addresses, UI mode, code location plus dependencies, and the storage location so the agent can update that section.

The IMPLIED section derives a structured, addressable record containing target address(es), scale/level, mode, captured content plus surrounding context, an editable source location, attachments, intent, author/timestamp/provenance, and a reply-thread root.

The ADDITIONS section proposes intent tags, priority, links to related envelopes, a proposed-change diff preview, status lifecycle, supervisor routing, target versioning for staleness detection, and audit-trail usage.
No concrete schema, example envelope, or validation rules are included.
standouts: The central idea is a self-resolving, addressable envelope that points directly to an editable location so an agent can act in place rather than search.
It conflates three roles in one unit: user annotation, supervisor routing packet, and audit/provenance record.
The SPECIFIED/IMPLIED/ADDITIONS structure mixes observed requirements, inferred structure, and aspirational features without clear binding strength.
conventions: Follows the channel-memory noticeboard frontmatter schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history.
Uses a breakdown note format with SPECIFIED, IMPLIED, and ADDITIONS sections.
Breaks the convention of purely factual records by treating inferred and proposed features as if they were derived from the stated goal.
concerns: SPECIFIED, IMPLIED, and ADDITIONS are not marked with binding strength, so mandatory, derived, and aspirational requirements are indistinguishable.
ADDITIONS is a broad feature list without prioritization, feasibility boundaries, or ownership.
No concrete schema, example envelope, or validation rules are provided.
Terms such as 'UI mode' and 'self-resolves' are used but not defined in this file.
notes: A design-breakdown item that attempts to define the 'context envelope' — the annotated, self-locating packet an agent would store, send, and act on.
questions: What is the exact schema and serialization format for the envelope?
How does the envelope 'self-resolve' an editable location across code://, board://, file path, and ui:// targets?
Which items in SPECIFIED, IMPLIED, and ADDITIONS are mandatory versus aspirational?
How is target versioning represented and how is staleness detected?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to define the context envelope; the file does that, but the actual content also expands into a broad wish-list of features, so the scope exceeds the stated breakdown.
intention_signals: It is trying to design a universal, self-contained annotation packet that removes search friction for an agent by bundling target, context, content, intent, and editable location into one routable, auditable unit.
novelty: The proposal that one addressable record should simultaneously be a user annotation, a supervisor routing unit, an in-place editable pointer, and an audit trail is unusual; it collapses communication, tasking, and provenance into a single envelope.
contribution: This file contributes a conceptual model and vocabulary — the context envelope, plus the SPECIFIED/IMPLIED/ADDITIONS breakdown style — for how annotations should be packaged so agents can act without additional search; it also surfaces cross-cutting concerns such as intent tagging, status lifecycle, target versioning, and audit trail that a fused system would need to reconcile.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard design-breakdown item defining the 'context envelope', a structured, addressable record that bundles an annotation, its annotated content, surrounding context, element IDs, UI mode, and editable source location so an agent can update the target directly.
intention_for_embedding: To specify the unit of stored and sent context for annotations so agents know what is meant, where it is, and how to update it, while also supporting routing, status tracking, versioning, and audit.
